flashcards.front: What are the five layers of Android architecture (bottom to top)?
flashcards.back:
1. Linux Kernel
2. Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL)
3. Native Libraries + Android Runtime (ART)
4. Application Framework
5. System Apps / Applications

flashcards.front: What role does the Linux Kernel play in Android?
flashcards.back: It is the foundation layer providing hardware drivers, process management, memory management, power management, security (user-based permissions and process isolation), and networking.

flashcards.front: What is the Hardware Abstraction Layer (HAL)?
flashcards.back: HAL provides standard interfaces that expose device hardware capabilities to the Java API framework. It contains vendor-specific modules for camera, audio, Bluetooth, sensors, and graphics.

flashcards.front: What is ART and how does it differ from Dalvik?
flashcards.back: ART (Android Runtime) replaced Dalvik in Android 5.0. ART uses Ahead-of-Time (AOT) + Just-in-Time (JIT) compilation for faster execution. Dalvik used JIT only. ART also has improved garbage collection.

flashcards.front: Name five native C/C++ libraries in Android.
flashcards.back:
1. SQLite (database)
2. OpenGL ES (graphics)
3. WebKit/Chromium (browser engine)
4. Media Framework (audio/video codecs)
5. Bionic libc (C standard library)

flashcards.front: What services does the Application Framework provide?
flashcards.back: Activity Manager, Window Manager, Content Providers, View System, Package Manager, Telephony Manager, Resource Manager, Location Manager, Notification Manager

flashcards.front: What is AOT compilation in ART?
flashcards.back: Ahead-of-Time compilation converts app bytecode (.dex) to native machine code at install time. This makes apps start faster and run more efficiently, though installation takes slightly longer.

flashcards.front: Why does Android use a modified Linux kernel?
flashcards.back: Linux provides open-source code, proven security model, process isolation, hardware driver support, shared libraries, memory management, and pre-emptive multitasking - all essential for a mobile OS.

flashcards.front: How do Android apps interact with hardware?
flashcards.back: Apps call Framework APIs, which communicate through HAL interfaces to vendor-specific HAL modules, which in turn use Linux kernel drivers to access physical hardware.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between system apps and user apps?
flashcards.back: System apps are pre-installed (Phone, Camera, Browser) and can be used by other apps via Intents. User apps are installed from Play Store, run in isolated sandboxes with unique Linux user IDs.
